title: Mapping Relations: Zero-One Matrices to Directed Graphs
description: A visual representation showing how a binary relation can be equivalently represented as both a zero-one matrix and a directed graph, and how each format is used for computer recognition and analysis.
relationships: A binary relation defined on sets A and B is a set of ordered pairs
The zero-one matrix is a tabular representation where 1 indicates a relation exists between row and column elements
The directed graph represents elements as nodes and relations as directed edges
Both representations are computationally equivalent and used for algorithms like path finding and closure properties
